observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the (a) Municipal
Assessor’s Office, Accounting Office, and Treasurer’s Office to maintain constant
coordination for the regular updating and reconciliation of their respective records on
RPT and SET; (b) Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
correct the balances of the RPT and SET receivable accounts, along with the
corresponding liability accounts, as warranted; and (c) Municipal Treasurer’s Office
intensify efforts to enhance RPT collection by implementing more proactive and
strategic collection measures.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) continue the close monitoring
of the granting and liquidation of cash advances and ensure strict adherence to the
pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; (b) conduct a thorough review of
the subsidiary ledgers to identify and rectify all incorrectly classified transactions;
and (c) reinforce the proper application of the Revised Chart of Accounts as
provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure the reliability of the
Municipality’s financial records and reports.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) create an Inventory Committee (IC) ; and (b)
instruct the (i) IC to conduct the physical count of inventories at least every six months
and prepare the RPCI and RPCSP for the inventory counted; (ii) Municipal Accountant to
(1) observe perpetual inventory and maintain necessary records in accounting for
these assets; and (2) coordinate with the MTO for the regular reconciliation of
inventory balances; (iii) Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required records and
reports for inventories.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Inventory Committee to (i)
conduct physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality; (ii) submit the RPCPPE to the
Audit Team on or before January 31 of each year; and (iii) reconcile the inventory
reports with the accounting records; (b) Municipal Treasurer to maintain PC and PAR for
the proper control and monitoring of the LGU’s PPE; (c) Municipal Accountant to
maintain PPELC; (d) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) consider
adopting the one-time cleansing option for PPE accounts; and (ii) in coordination with
the accountable officers, prepare IIRUP to report all unserviceable properties; and (d)
Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable PPEs in accordance
with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the implementing and concerned offices,
ensure continued adherence to the terms and conditions of current and future fund
transfers from National Government Agencies to maximize fund utilization and achieve
intended program outcomes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal
Accounting Office to sustain its efforts in fully adopting IPSAS 39 - Employee
Benefits; and (b) Human Resource Management Office to continue to
maintain and update the Municipality’s employees’ records.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accounting Office to
(a) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reverse the reclassification of the still
unidentified PPE items; and (b) adhere to the required disclosure requirements for
all relevant accounts and transactions to enhance transparency and financial
statement reliability.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) verify and assess the noted tax
deficiencies of the eight identified business taxpayers, and take appropriate actions
thereon; (b) enforce the timely submission of ITR for all business taxpayers
regardless of business size; and (c) exercise the authority granted under Section
171 of RA No. 7160 for more accurate tax assessments.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Development Council
Executive Committee to strengthen the planning of the PPAs to ensure their timely
implementation; (b) BAC, TWG and Municipal Accountant to (i) submit all the
requisite documents; and (ii) strictly comply with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184
and its IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurements; and (c) Municipal
Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of
procurements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the HRMO to:
(a) conduct a comprehensive study to develop an Organizational Structure and
Staffing Pattern Plan, in order to determine the ideal number and composition of
plantilla positions based on the municipality’s operational needs;
(b) formulate policies and guidelines to assess the necessity and propriety of
hiring Job Order (JO) and Contract of Service (CoS) personnel, taking into
consideration the functions and capacity of existing regular employees;
(c) implement a more robust internal control system for recruitment and
selection processes to ensure transparency, accuracy of applicant information, and
compliance with employment standards; and
(d) ensure that the actual tasks performed by JOs and CoS personnel are
consistent with the duties and responsibilities specified in their respective
contracts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) LDRRM Office to (i) sustain the high
implementation and utilization rates of PPAs and the LDRRMF to maximize their impact
on disaster preparedness and response; (ii) ensure the timely and accurate submission of
monthly utilization reports to oversight agencies; (iii) strictly implement all PPAs in
accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract to prevent delays and ensure
project quality; and (b) Inspection Officer to ensure strict adherence to proper inspection
protocols before issuing the IAR to prevent the premature release of payments for
incomplete or non-compliant deliveries.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to sustain and further strengthen its
efforts in promoting and institutionalizing gender mainstreaming in local
policy-making, budgeting, implementation, and reporting, in compliance with PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMCs No. 2013-01 and 2016-01.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accounting and
Treasury Offices to sustain the timely withholding and remittance of taxes in
accordance with pertinent regulations.

We commended Management for the sustained observance of the GSIS Act of 1997,
and advised to which they agreed to continue the withholding and timely remittance
of all premium contributions and loan amortizations of its employees as well as the
government’s share to the fund.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accounting and
Treasury Offices to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home
Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accounting and
Treasury Offices to continue the very good practice of promptly remitting all
collections due to PhilHealth.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the named person liable
for the Notice of Disallowance to comply immediately with the requirements stated
in the said Notice.

cleansing option for PPE accounts; and (ii) in coordination with the accountable
officers, prepare IIRUP to report all unserviceable properties; and (d) Disposal
Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable PPEs in accordance
with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

2.11. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) properly prepare and maintain
complete and updated SCs and SLCs, respectively, and (ii) reconcile their
inventory records regularly; (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) receive delivered
items, prepare AIR and sign the acceptance portion thereof as required by
Section 122(f) of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and (ii) prepare
SSMI and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as the basis of the latter
in preparing JEV for issuance of inventories; (c) Requesting Units to prepare
RIS for every requisition of supplies; (d) Municipal Accountant to use SSMI as
basis in recognizing supplies and materials expense; and (e) Inventory
Committee to (i) conduct physical count of supplies and materials semestrally;
and (ii) report the same in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) for submission to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31
of each year.

3.19. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal
Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), Acting Municipal Engineer, and
Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to strictly
observe the time frame for the implementation/completion of the projects to
ensure efficient delivery of public services to the intended beneficiaries; (b)
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized
funds to the respective source agencies and Bureau of Treasury together with the
requisite report of disbursement in observance of COA Circular No. 94-013; and
(c) Municipal Accountant to seek confirmation from the agencies concerned for
the dormant balances of fund transfers, and dispose the same in accordance with
the terms of the MOA and applicable rules and regulations.

4.18. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MPDO and Acting
Municipal Engineer to (a) ascertain the viability of each planned project to
ensure the efficient use of the fund; (b) see to it that all PPAs included in the
20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement- and
implementation-ready in accordance with Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1; and (c) regularly monitor/supervise project
implementation to allow timely interventions on concerns that may arise
therefrom.

5.29. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) LDRRMO, in
coordination with the Acting Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned,
to maximize the use of LDRRMF through facilitated implementation of the
programmed PPAs within the budget year; (b) LDRRMO and Acting Municipal
Engineer to facilitate the implementation of the six projects that had remained
unimplemented in CY 2023; (c) LDRRM Council to endeavor to attain sound
planning of project implementation to minimize instances of fund realignments
and reprogramming; (d) BAC to see to it that transparency and accountability
on procurement processes are promoted through the posting of required
information; and (e) Municipal Accountant to submit all procurement-related
documents required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and R.A. No. 9184 and its
RIRR to support the regularity of the payments made.

6.18. We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the MENRO to (i) in
coordination with Barangay Officials, consider the inclusion of vermicomposting
operation in every MRF and ensure that they are fully operating and functioning
to help in the diversion of biodegradable wastes; (ii) make representation with
the Municipal Budget Office for the purchase of additional recycling machineries
to help in the solid waste reduction and diversion; (iii) implement fully the
mandatory waste segregation at source as one of the strategies mentioned in the
Ten-Year SWMP; and (iv) strictly enforce penalties provided in the
Municipality’s Revised Environment Code for anti-littering ordinance violators;
and (b) consider purchasing or providing additional garbage truck to augment
existing facilities in collecting residual wastes from the transfer stations/MRFs.

7.22. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) BPLO and
Municipal Treasurer to verify and assess the noted tax deficiencies of the three
identified business taxpayers, and take appropriate actions thereon; (b) BPLO to
(i) align their licensing procedures with the RLRC and enforce the timely
submission of ITR for all business taxpayers regardless of business size; (ii)
impose fines on businesses that are operating but failed to secure or renew their
business permits in accordance with Section 88 of RLRC; and (iii) henceforth
ensure that all businesses operating in the locality have valid business permits;
and (c) Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the proper office for
the hiring of additional personnel or assignment of staff who will be trained and
assigned in the examination of books of accounts of businesses.

8.20. We recommended and the LCE agreed that the MCEDO collaborate with
government agencies concerned to find ways: (a) on how to make the existing
PGs to be more competitive and relevant in the light of the evolving market
landscape; (b) to raise or provide financial and technical support to sustain the
PGs’ operations; and (c) ensure the continuous monitoring of I-Reap subprojects
to allow timely interventions and assistance with the view of achieving long-term
viability of the projects for the benefits of the farmers and PGs.

9.11. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer
to (a) inform the Audit Team that appropriate signboards and/or public notices
are already posted before the start of each infrastructure project; (b) provide
proof of installation of signboards for the 25 completed projects; otherwise,
cause the refund of the cost of the said signboards equivalent to the value
indicated in the approved POWs; and (c) ensure that the appropriate signboard
containing the information of future infrastructure projects is displayed in
conspicuous places within the Municipality premises, and in the venue where the
PPA is located or carried out as required by Item 2.2.1 of COA Circular No.
2013-004.

10.9. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Bids and Awards
Committee and Head, BAC Secretariat to submit copies of Contract Agreements
for infrastructure projects and all documents forming part thereof to the Audit
Team within five days from its execution as required by COA Circular No. 2009-
001.
Management’s comment:
10.10. In reply to our AOM and during the exit conference, the MPDO commented
that they are submitting copies of contract to the Accounting Office within five days
from its execution with only few instances of late submission. Nevertheless, he
admitted their lapses of not directly submitting the copies to the Audit Team as
required by the regulation and had agreed to follow the audit recommendation.

11.13. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the GFPS through the
GAD Focal Person to (a) adhere with the guidelines for reporting changes made
to the approved and endorsed GPB to the concerned DILG office; (b) prioritize
the conduct of gender sensitivity training to strengthen the capacity of the GFPS
members; and (c) continue its efforts in promoting and ensuring gender
mainstreaming in local policy making, planning, budgeting, implementation, and
monitoring as required by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMCs No. 2013-01 and
2016-01.

12.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) ensure the full remittance of the
withheld amounts within the prescribed period; and (b) continuously comply
with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and timely remittance of
taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes, and expanded taxes
from government money payments.

13.6. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Human Resources
and Management Office (HRMO) in coordination with the Accounting Office to
(a) make representations with the CSC for the facilitation of the review of the
appointment papers of newly-hired employees and accordingly remit the
corresponding premiums, as warranted; and (b) sustain the timely withholding
and remittance of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations of members in
accordance with Section 6(b) of Republic Act No. 8291.

14.5. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) HRMO in
coordination with the Accounting Office to (i) make representations with the
CSC for the facilitation of the review of the appointment papers of newly-hired
employees and accordingly remit the corresponding premiums, as warranted; (ii)
cause the refund of over-withheld contributions to employees; and (iii) sustain
the timely withholding and remittance of PhilHealth premiums of members in
accordance with R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the
National Health Insurance Act of 2013; and (b) Municipal Accountant to draw
journal entry to correct the year-end balances of Due to PhilHealth, Due to
Officers and Employees and Government Equity accounts.

15.5. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer agreed to continue the timely withholding and remittance to HDMF of
the members’ contributions and loan amortization, together with the LGU’s
share, in accordance with R.A. No. 9679.

16.6. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the named person liable
for the Notice of Disallowance to comply immediately with the requirements stated
in the said Notice.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) BPLO to (i) be

examine and verify the books of accounts of and business establishments; and (c)

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal Engineer

observations.managementAction:
The DSWD, as the primary source of the fund for ESA, did not provide the Supplemental Memorandum of Agreement, instead Specific Implementation Arrangements was provided.

There are pictures of actual pay-out integrated in the Terminal Report and copy of the Cash Assistance Payroll duly signed by the beneficiaries as a proof that they have received the amount stated therein.

RA 9184 was not applicable for this project since “Procurement” was not stated in MOA as well as in the Specific Implementation Arrangement. Furthermore, the MSWD Office did not participate in any procurement of construction materials made by the beneficiaries. The beneficiaries were the one who procured the construction materials with their own choice of suppliers and since they have different needs. After the pay-out, MSWD Office just monitored the beneficiaries to make sure that they will use the money they received for the rehabilitation of their houses, thus  the acquisition of construction materials.

The management strictly complied with the manner in which the ESA were distributed to the beneficiaries.
observations.reason:
Interviews with actual beneficiaries revealed that they were not the one in-charge of purchasing said construction materials and that they were “informed” that part of the monies they received will be withheld for the purchasing of the construction materials to be delivered to them.

“Procurement” of construction materials were not included in the MOA, however, the manner of the dole out chose by the municipality based on actual validation of the Audit Team included using a portion of the fund in purchasing of construction materials as narrated by the actual beneficiaries during the conduct of project validation. 



Explanation of management did not equate in the manner of actual distribution as per validation/survey of the Audit Team on selected beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Members of the Local School Board (a) ensure that future plans and budgets for SEF are consistent with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 and other pertinent rules that may be issued thereafter; and (b) refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.  Likewise, we also advised that the Municipal Accountant submit the documents found lacking in the identified disbursements and ensure that the required documents were attached to future payments.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) all heads of offices concerned to include the PPMPs of all projects, programs and activities that will be prioritized by the Municipality for a given year; (b) the BAC Secretariat to consolidate these requirements in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) that is compliant with the required format; (c) the Engineering Office and MPDC to (i) ensure that the requisite conditions and authority are met before implementing projects by administration;   and (ii) coordinate in the planning for, implementation and monitoring of said projects; and (d) the Accounting Office to ascertain completeness of the required documents before payments related to said projects are made. Likewise, we advised, which Management conformed, that the LCE formally designate all key personnel in project implementation and clearly define their duties and responsibilities to avoid confusion and miscommunication.

We recommended that the management instruct the MSWDO to (a) secure the signed and notarized Supplemental Memorandum of Agreement from the DSWD Regional Office III; (b) provide the reports of assistances showing the actual cash and construction materials given to the beneficiaries; (c) provide justification for not procuring the construction materials through public bidding in compliance with the R.A. 9184; and (d) present the explanation on the discrepancies noted above in the manner on which the “Emergency Shelter Assistance” Program was carried out.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) the OIC- Human Resource Management Officer to clearly define in the contract of service the duties and responsibilities of Job Order Personnel to avoid confusion and duplication of duties; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents, such as accomplishment reports for services rendered, were attached to the disbursements vouchers to establish validity and propriety of payment.

We recommended that the management maximize the appropriated amount for implementation of projects and programs that will attain the objectives for Solid Waste Management.  Likewise, we reiterated our previous year recommendation that the MSWMB involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws; intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and exercise strong political will to implement the laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) provide the required training interventions for the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to aid them in the proper discharge of their functions; and (b) instruct said committee and group to review the GAD Plan and Budget to determine programs, projects, and activities that are responsive to gender gaps or issues faced by their women and men employees, as well as their clients and constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) convene the LCPC; and (b) instruct the (i) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer in coordination with the LCPC to prepare and formulate programs and activities for the attainment of the program objective of the RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120; and (ii) Municipal Budget Officer to provide for the 1% LCPC funding allocation in their CY 2018 Annual Budget, and every year thereafter.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Human Resource Management Officer, in the preparation of payrolls, to observe the limitation on net take home pay as set forth in the GAA; and (b) officials and employees of the Municipality to observe strictly the rules and regulations regarding the required employee’s monthly net take home pay.  Management should also deny processing of employees’ loan applications if it will bring their net take home pay below P4,000.00 per month.
We recommended that the Municipality continue to comply with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended that the Municipality continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended that the Municipality continue to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

We recommended the Management to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009, unremitted contribution will be remitted on the following month.

We recommended the settlement of the audit disallowances in accordance with the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA). Require all those named liable in the Notice of Disallowance to refund the amount disallowed.
